summary: Observed Explorer runtime setting for always showing icons instead of thumbnails. Microsoft Open Specifications maps alwaysShowIcons to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\IconsOnly and defines 1 as enabled and 0 as disabled, the 25H2 raw registry dump and 25H2 default-user hive corroborate the same value family, and a reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\IconsOnly with both Data: 0 and Data: 1 after Explorer restart.

setting.name: Always Show Icons, Never Thumbnails
setting.casual_explanation: Explorer can show file icons instead of generating thumbnails.
setting.why_people_change_it: People enable this when they want faster or simpler icon-based browsing, or disable it when they want thumbnail previews for images and media.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: This is a low-risk Explorer preference. Enabling it replaces thumbnails with icons, while disabling it allows thumbnail previews where Explorer supports them.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Open Specifications maps the alwaysShowIcons option to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\IconsOnly and defines 1 as enabled and 0 as disabled. The 25H2 raw registry dump lists IconsOnly under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch and the siloed user branches, while the 25H2 default-user hive sets IconsOnly = 0. A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon probe on 2026-03-24 then confirmed live Explorer consumption: after writing IconsOnly = 0 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the value with Data: 0; after writing IconsOnly = 1 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the same value with Data: 1.

allowed_values.label: Allow thumbnails
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer allows thumbnail previews instead of forcing icons only. Microsoft documents 0 as the disabled state, the 25H2 default-user hive sets IconsOnly = 0, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 0 after restart.

allowed_values.label: Always show icons, never thumbnails
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer forces icons instead of thumbnails. Microsoft documents 1 as the enabled state, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 1 after restart.

targets.notes: This record validates the documented 0/1 semantics against the live Explorer runtime surface on Win25H2Clean.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now exposes IconsOnly through VisibilityTweakProvider and writes the same HKCU Explorer\Advanced value that the runtime probe validated.

windows_defaults.label: 25H2 default-user baseline

states.rationale: HKCU25H2.reg sets IconsOnly = 0 in the 25H2 default-user hive.

windows_defaults.notes: The validated VM user baseline differed from the default-user hive, but the 25H2 default profile still corroborates the expected default state.

recommended_profiles.label: Allow thumbnails
intended_for: Users who want preview thumbnails in Explorer

states.rationale: This matches the documented disabled state, the 25H2 default-user hive, and the runtime-observed Explorer read.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want thumbnail previews in Explorer.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a simpler icon-only view.
tradeoffs: Allows thumbnail previews where Explorer supports them.
May show richer previews instead of a simpler icon-only view.

recommended_profiles.label: Always show icons, never thumbnails
avoid_for: Users who rely on thumbnail previews

states.rationale: This matches the documented enabled state and the runtime-observed Explorer read.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Explorer to avoid thumbnail previews.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want media and image thumbnails visible.
tradeoffs: Forces icon-only presentation instead of thumbnails.

evidence.title: Microsoft Open Specifications: GlobalFolderOptionsVista alwaysShowIcons
evidence.summary: Microsoft maps alwaysShowIcons to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\IconsOnly and states that it MUST be 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

evidence.title: 25H2 raw registry and default-hive corroboration for IconsOnly
evidence.summary: The 25H2 raw registry dump lists IconsOnly under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch and the siloed user branches, and the 25H2 default-user hive sets IconsOnly = 0.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Explorer IconsOnly runtime surface
evidence.summary: A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 wrote IconsOnly = 0 and IconsOnly = 1, restarted Explorer after each write, and captured Explorer.EXE querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\IconsOnly with matching Data: 0 and Data: 1 values.

decision.why: Microsoft Open Specifications documents the exact IconsOnly path and 0/1 semantics, the 25H2 dump/default hive corroborate the value family on current builds, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon trace confirms live Explorer consumption of both states. This is a low-risk user-scope Explorer preference.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: STATE=0 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\IconsOnly ... Data: 0; STATE=1 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\IconsOnly ... Data: 1; RESTORED_EXISTS=True RESTORED_VALUE=1
validation_proof.notes: The guest-local result file was copied back to the host scratch area during validation. The VM user baseline was 1 and was restored after the probe.
